title: NOC 72102 – Sheet metal workers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 72102 covers the skilled tradespeople who fabricate, assemble, install, and repair sheet metal products. As a TEER 2 occupation, these professionals are essential to Canada's construction and manufacturing sectors, working on everything from high-efficiency HVAC systems and metal roofing to specialized components for the aerospace and food processing industries. In 2026, as Canada mandates stricter "Green Building" codes and energy-efficient ventilation, Sheet Metal Workers are at the forefront of the country's infrastructure modernization. For immigration, this role is a priority for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and Category-Based Express Entry draws.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level2Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group721 – Machining, metal forming, shaping and erecting tradesMinor Group7210 – Machining, metal forming, shaping and erecting tradesNOC Code72102

duties: Fabrication: Read and interpret blueprints, sketches, and product specifications to determine layout and sequence of operations.
Layout: Use laser or computerized equipment to lay out, measure, and mark sheet metal according to drawings.
Machine Operation: Operate light and heavy machinery, such as shears, brakes, punches, and CNC plasma cutters, to cut, bend, and straighten sheet metal.
Assembly: Fit and join sheet metal parts using riveting, welding, soldering, and specialized fasteners to create ducts, hoppers, and casings.
Installation: Install fabricated components in buildings, such as air conditioning ducts, furnace casings, and exterior metal siding.
Inspection: Inspect product quality and installation to ensure they meet building codes and client specifications.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a 3-to-5-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over 4 years of work experience and industry-certified courses.

Certification (Trade Seal): * Trade certification is compulsory in Ontario, Alberta, Saskatchewan, and Quebec.Trade certification is available (but voluntary) in all other provinces and territories.Highly recommended to obtain the Red Seal Endorsement, which allows for interprovincial mobility and is a mark of excellence for PR applications.
Trade certification is available (but voluntary) in all other provinces and territories.
Highly recommended to obtain the Red Seal Endorsement, which allows for interprovincial mobility and is a mark of excellence for PR applications.
Proficiency in CAD software (AutoCAD/Revit) for layout is increasingly required for lead-hand roles in 2026.
salary_prospects: Note: Journeypersons working on specialized industrial or unionized commercial projects often earn annual salaries exceeding $95,000 - $115,000 inclusive of overtime.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), the sector will face a significant need for new workers due to the transition to net-zero buildings. The requirement for specialized ductwork for high-efficiency ventilation and complex metal roofing systems ensures that Sheet Metal Workers will remain in high demand through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Sheet Metal Workers is Very Good, with a persistent shortage of journeypersons across the country. In 2026, Ontario, British Columbia, and Saskatchewan are reporting "High Demand" indices due to a massive backlog in residential high-rise construction and the expansion of industrial clean-tech facilities. With a high volume of upcoming retirements, the Canadian government has identified this trade as a "Key Shortage Area," making it easier for qualified international workers to secure LMIA-supported job offers.
Green Tech Transition: Specialize in the installation of advanced heat recovery ventilators (HRV) and sustainable metal cladding systems.
Diverse Work Environments: Choose between shop-based fabrication using high-tech CNC plasma cutters or field-based installation on major commercial sites.
Strong Union Support: Many sheet metal workers in Canada benefit from strong union representation (e.g., SMART), ensuring top-tier wages and pension plans.
Strategic PR Pathways: This trade is explicitly listed in Express Entry Category-Based Selection for "Trade Occupations," often requiring lower CRS scores for an Invitation to Apply (ITA).
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Targeted under the Trade Occupations category in 2026.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): The primary federal route for tradespeople with at least 2 years of experience.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: A high-conversion route for workers currently employed in the province’s booming construction sector.
BC PNP Skilled Worker: Priority for sheet metal workers supporting the province's massive housing and infrastructure projects.
